items: The maintenance precautions specific to each model or common to all models are listed in the "Precautions" section. Follow these precautions to ensure safe and reliable maintenance. The Honda Service Manual is compiled for people who have basic skills and knowledge of Honda vehicle maintenance. If a person who does not have these skills and knowledge works according to the manual procedures, he/she may not be able to perform correct maintenance.

When performing maintenance, be sure to refer to the related items and precautions and follow the work procedures. Failure to follow the correct procedures may result in vehicle damage, accidents, etc.
When working on airbag system related components, refer to the "Precautions" and "Configuration Diagram" of the SRS airbag system as well.

Parts requiring caution when disposing of ​

refs.label: Parts requiring caution when disposing of

items.text: Whenever handling parts of the SRS airbag system, be sure to check the relevant items before proceeding.
Never disassemble.
Store in a hard, flat location away from high temperatures and light sources.
Do not allow oil, grease, washing oil, water, etc. to adhere to the product.
When disposing of SRS-related parts, be sure to activate (deploy) them before disposal.
When disposing of the product, dispose of it in accordance with each prefectural ordinance.
Be careful not to give any impact to the sensor or unit.
Never perform electrical inspections such as resistance measurements.
When performing sheet metal paint repairs, the precautions in the body maintenance section should be followed.

blocks.text: Labels with precautions specific to the SRS airbag system are attached to these locations.

items.text: Caution when removing and installing the fuel tube with quick connectors

refs.label: Caution when removing and installing the fuel tube with quick connectors

items.text: Whenever engine/fuel emission control-related maintenance is performed, be sure to check the related items before starting work.
When removing and installing the fuel piping system, remove fuel pressure before proceeding.
Work shall be performed under strict fire prevention.
Work in a well-ventilated area.
Prepare a fire extinguisher before work.
Never use anything with flying sparks nearby.
When installing radios and other equipment that generate powerful radio waves, check the relevant items before proceeding.
Reacts with water and oxygen in the air. In particular, it reacts violently with water and may cause an explosion, so handle and dispose of with care.
Protective eyewear (goggles) and gloves (rubber) must be worn when working with the product, as contact with the human body may result in burns, and contact with the eyes may result in blindness.
Work in a well ventilated area because hydrogen gas is generated when reacting with water.
If it comes in contact with the human body, rinse with plenty of water and seek immediate medical attention.

blocks.text: A. If the valve is not damaged

items.text: Do not intentionally damage the valve to remove metallic sodium.
As long as the metallic sodium sealed in the valve is not exposed to air, the valve can be handled in the same way as an ordinary valve.
If it is clearly known that the industrial waste treatment method is not a pressing process but dissolution, the treatment may be requested from an industrial waste contractor.
If it is clearly known that the industrial waste treatment method is not a pressing process but dissolution, the treatment may be requested from an industrial waste contractor.

blocks.text: B. If the valve is damaged

items.text: Be sure to wear protective goggles and gloves (rubber) and work in a well-ventilated area.
Prepare a fire extinguisher (powder) nearby.
Neutralization operations must be performed before industrial disposal. ​

refs.label: Neutralization operations must be performed before industrial disposal.

items.text: Do not touch a damaged valve directly with your hand using tongs/tweezers, etc.
If the reaction solution gets into eyes, rinse immediately with a large amount of water and seek medical advice. If it gets on skin, rinse immediately with a large amount of water.

blocks.text: Prepare a container with at least 10 liters of water.
2. Submerge the damaged valve in the container. 3. When the reaction is finished (4-5 hours), remove the parts with tongs/tweezers, etc. and discard them as you would any other parts.

items.text: Damaged valves shall be completely submerged.
Keep fire away from the product as hydrogen gas is generated.
Due to the intense reactions, stay at least 2-3 m away.
No more than six bottles should be processed at a time.
Since the reaction liquid is a strong alkaline liquid (sodium hydroxide), disposal should be in accordance with local regulations.
Remove old liquid packing, oil and dirt from application surfaces, bolts and bolt holes.
The liquid packing shall be applied evenly and without breaks to the application surface according to the application volume instructions.
The application position should be in the center of the applied surface. Around the bolt, the application should be made inside to prevent oil and other substances from leaking.
Liquid packing shall be done on one side of the surface without female thread machining.
Do not apply liquid packing to the groove of the O-ring.
If the product is left for more than 5 minutes after application, do not install it as it is. Remove the coating once and reapply.
Oil should be injected at least 30 minutes after installation.
Idling Stop System Operating Conditions

When inspecting or servicing the engine compartment, the ignition key should be removed or the smart key should be kept away from the vehicle to prevent the engine from restarting, as restarting the engine may cause an unexpected accident.
If the idling stop system is activated during the vehicle inspection process, the vehicle must be restarted and inspected after referring to the list of conditions that prohibit idling stop systems.
Attention specific to this model (4WD)
Whenever performing maintenance related to the 4WD system, be sure to check the relevant items before performing the work.
When performing the speedometer test, a free roller or similar device should be used.
When performing inspections, etc., that require the wheels to rotate, be sure to lift up all four wheels so that they can rotate freely before performing the inspection.
Refrigerant cycle maintenance precautions ​
Auto Air Conditioner Troubleshooting Procedure
Whenever performing refrigerant cycle-related maintenance, be sure to check the related items before performing the work.
Refrigerant must always be used in a refrigerant recovery machine and must not be released into the atmosphere.
The air conditioner gas must be drained before any work is performed.
Audio & Visual Systems Work Precautions
Audio & Visual System Troubleshooting Procedure
When LCDs are exposed to direct sunlight or other high temperatures, they may not display true black when the NAV screen is activated.
Never rub the LCD surface strongly by hand or with a hard cloth. When removing dirt, wipe lightly with a cloth for wiping glasses for plastic lenses.

The electrostatic touch panel used for the LCD and face switches (AUDIO, HOME, MENU, VOL+, VOL-, and BACK buttons) will not respond to gloves that are 2.5 mm or thicker or gloves made of insulating materials.
Protecting the vehicle.
Use work covers (fender covers, seat covers, floor covers, steering wheel covers, etc.) as necessary to prevent staining or damage to painted surfaces, seats, etc.
Use work covers (fender covers, seat covers, floor covers, steering wheel covers, etc.) as necessary to prevent staining or damage to painted surfaces, seats, etc.
Safe working practices.
Whenever the vehicle is jacked up, the rigid rack must be in place.
Whenever the vehicle is jacked up, the rigid rack must be in place.
In the case of joint work performed by two or more people, both parties should check each other's safety.
Be careful about ventilation when working with the engine running.
Accurate work shall be performed for removal and disassembly.

After confirming the location of the defect and at the same time determining the cause of the defect and ascertaining whether removal or disassembly is necessary, work shall be performed according to the procedures indicated in the manual.

After confirming the location of the defect and at the same time determining the cause of the defect and ascertaining whether removal or disassembly is necessary, work shall be performed according to the procedures indicated in the manual.
When removing or disassembling, the proper assembly condition should be confirmed before starting.
Each time a part is removed, inspect the condition in which it was installed and whether it is deformed, damaged, or scratched.
When disassembling a part with a large number of parts, similar parts, or parts identical on both sides, the parts shall be organized to avoid confusion during assembly.
Special tools must be used for work that requires them.
Substituting work with other tools may result in damage to parts or injury, so be sure to use dedicated tools for work for which you are instructed to use them.
Substituting work with other tools may result in damage to parts or injury, so be sure to use dedicated tools for work for which you are instructed to use them.
Observe established maintenance standards.
About Tightening Torque
Tightening of bolts, nuts, and screws shall be done with the specified torque in a diagonal line from inside to outside, from the larger diameter to the smaller diameter.
Tightening of bolts, nuts, and screws shall be done with the specified torque in a diagonal line from inside to outside, from the larger diameter to the smaller diameter.
Use of Special Tools and Extension Tools

If the effective length is changed by connecting a special tool or extension tool to the torque wrench, the actual torque applied to the tightening area will be greater than the torque wrench reading.

If the effective length is changed by connecting a special tool or extension tool to the torque wrench, the actual torque applied to the tightening area will be greater than the torque wrench reading.
Be sure to check the length of the tool to be used and tighten with the torque wrench reading calculated by the following formula.
Torque wrenches and special tools must be used in a straight line. If they are not in a straight line, the specified torque will not be applied.
Replacement Parts
Gaskets, packings, O-rings, split pins, lock washers, self-locking nuts, etc. shall be replaced with new ones upon disassembly.
Gaskets, packings, O-rings, split pins, lock washers, self-locking nuts, etc. shall be replaced with new ones upon disassembly.
About Parts
Always use Honda genuine parts or recommended parts for parts, oil and grease.
Always use Honda genuine parts or recommended parts for parts, oil and grease.
Parts to be reused shall be inspected and measured in detail as necessary.
About Oils and Fats
When assembling and installing, apply the specified oil and grease to the designated areas.
When assembling and installing, apply the specified oil and grease to the designated areas.
Each component to be reused should be thoroughly cleaned and washed.

When applying silicone grease, be careful not to adhere to the surrounding switch and coupler terminals. Do not touch the terminals of switches and couplers with hands or gloves that are covered with silicone grease.
When applying a spray containing silicone, cover the surrounding switch and coupler terminals with a cloth to prevent splashing.
Brake Fluid and Brake Related Parts
Do not allow debris or water to contaminate the brake fluid when refilling.
Do not allow debris or water to contaminate the brake fluid when refilling.
To prevent chemical changes, do not use different brands of brake fluid.
Do not reuse drained brake fluid.
Do not allow brake fluid to adhere to painted surfaces. If it does adhere, wash it off with water immediately.
Keep hose (or pipe) joints covered to prevent brake fluid from spilling out.
Disassembled parts should be cleaned with brake fluid and checked for ventilation at each port by pressurized air.
Disassembled parts should be organized to keep them free of dust and debris.
Make sure that each component is free of dust and dirt before assembly.
Parts that are designated for replacement at disassembly or periodic replacement must be replaced.
After assembly, be sure to inspect the tightness, operation, piping condition, and clamp position of each part.
Handling of rubber parts
Rubber parts should be inspected for deterioration or damage upon removal or disassembly, and replaced if necessary.
Rubber parts should be inspected for deterioration or damage upon removal or disassembly, and replaced if necessary.

Some rubber parts are altered when gasoline, oil, or cleaning oil adheres to them. Rubber parts that are not instructed to be coated with oil or grease should be handled in such a way that oil or grease does not adhere to them, and if it does adhere, the oil or grease should be wiped off immediately.
Rubber Hose and Tubing Removal and Installation

When removing the rubber hose (or tube), use a flat-blade screwdriver with a rounded tip to press down on the end face of the hose while removing it. Do not use sharp tools to pry or forcibly pull on the hose to remove it.

When removing the rubber hose (or tube), use a flat-blade screwdriver with a rounded tip to press down on the end face of the hose while removing it. Do not use sharp tools to pry or forcibly pull on the hose to remove it.
Insert the hose securely up to the joint bulge or stopper. If the hose has bulge marks on it, insert it in line with the bulge.

When reconnecting the hose, install the clamps according to the clamp marks on the hose. For new hoses, 4-5 mm from hose end The clamps shall be securely mounted in the position indicated in the table below. However, if installation instructions for the clamp are provided, the clamp shall be installed in accordance with these instructions.
In the case of screw-type clamps, do not forcefully tighten the clamp.
Clamps that are deformed or sagging should be replaced with new ones. Also, hoses with loose joints and hose attachments should be replaced.
When bulk parts (1m, 3m, 8m long wound products) hoses and tubes are used as substitutes for standard parts, the following details shall be followed.
Bulk parts with parentheses ( ) listed under the part name of the standard part in the parts list.
Bulk parts with parentheses ( ) listed under the part name of the standard part in the parts list.
Refer to the parts list or cut bulk parts to regular lengths to match the standard parts of the current item.
Cautions for disconnection

If the part number of the standard part has a tubing mark indication (code for part number), the tubing mark shall be marked on the cut tube using an oil-based pen. The tubing mark should be selected by checking the part number and selecting the mark according to Table 1, or the mark should be the same as the actual mark on the standard part.
After use, remaining bulk parts should be stored out of direct sunlight and away from high temperatures.

Aluminum engine, transmission, or suspension parts may use special bolts and nuts. Therefore, when replacing bolts and nuts, be sure to check the part number in the parts list and use genuine Honda parts.
Always disconnect the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery before any work is performed. 12V battery Caution when canceling The work should also be performed with certainty by referring also to the
Upon completion of the work, reconfirm how each part is connected, secured, and threaded.
If a fuse blows, always replace it with a fuse of the specified capacity after investigating the cause and repairing it.
Covers shall be securely placed over terminals after work is completed.

blocks.text: When disconnecting or connecting couplers, the following items should be taken into consideration

items.text: With the exception of the 1P connector, all are push-down type locking couplers. Therefore, when disconnecting, remove the lock (A) before proceeding.

In the case of a coupler attached to a stay (B), a pull-up type lock is used at the stay and coupler attachment point. Some couplers of this type cannot be disconnected unless they are removed from the stay.

blocks.text: For the double-locking stay-mounted type, remove it from the stay (B) first and then disconnect the coupler.
Press the locking tab (A) to remove the coupler.
To remove from stay (A), pull up the locking tab.
To unlock the coupler, gently push in the coupler once (to give clearance to the locking section) to allow the claws (A) to move, then push the locking tab to release the coupler.

items.text: When disconnecting the coupler, hold the coupler body.
Do not pull on the wire harness.
The vinyl cover of the coupler must completely cover the coupler, making sure that it is not torn off.
Before connecting the coupler, make sure that there are no bent terminals, excessively protruding terminals, or disconnected terminals.

blocks.text: Confirm that there is no dislodging of retainers or seals. Next, indicate any abnormal conditions such as retainer (A) or seal (B) dislodging.
For couplers using insulating grease, if the amount of grease is insufficient or dirty, fill the coupler with insulating grease after cleaning.

items.text: The coupler must be fully inserted.
Ensure that the lock is fully secured.
Ensure that the wire harness is not disconnected.
There are two types of coupler connections: one is made by pressing down on the locking tab, and the other is made without touching the locking tab.
When connecting the coupler, push it all the way in and confirm that it is securely locked by a locking sound (click).
Before connecting connectors, make sure that there are no tears in the cover or over-opening of the female terminals.
Connectors must be fully inserted.
Ensure that the vinyl cover completely covers the terminals.
Do not point the opening of the vinyl cover upward.
Cable straps and harness bands must be securely attached.
Remove the locking tabs carefully to avoid damaging them.
Wire harnesses shall be clamped so that they do not come in contact with rotating, moving, or vibrating parts.
Wire harnesses shall be clamped so that they do not come in contact with parts that produce high heat.
Wire harnesses should be threaded so that they do not contact body edges or sharp edges.
Wire harnesses should be threaded so that they do not contact the head or tip of the bolt or screw.
Wire harnesses shall be free of slack or unreasonable pull.
If a wire harness must inevitably come into contact with an edge or sharp corner, the area should be protected with tubing or tape.
Plasters shall be applied in a manner that removes rust and oil adhesion and prevents peeling.
If the wire harness has grommets, they must be set securely and sealant must be used at the points indicated by the arrows.
Do not tear the sheath of the wire harness.
If the wire harness is torn, it should be repaired or replaced by wrapping it with vinyl tape.
Avoid wire harnesses becoming entangled when installing parts.
Do not twist the wire harness.
When using the tester, understand the instructions in the tester's instruction manual and work in accordance with the service manual.
When using a tester to inspect the coupler section, be sure to insert the tester rod from the wire harness side (except where waterproof couplers or aluminum wiring is used).
The tester rod should be tapered with a thin tip.
When inserting the tester rod, do not screw it in forcibly.
Do not move the inserted tester rod unnecessarily.
Do not drop or throw parts.
Observe the following precautions for locations where aluminum wiring is used to avoid corrosion and wire breakage.
Do not damage the harness with a tester rod as this will damage the corrosion protection coating.
Do not damage the harness with a tester rod as this will damage the corrosion protection coating.
Do not disassemble the coupler.
Do not disassemble the coupler.
Do not repair/modify the harness and always replace it if there is any abnormality.
Do not repair/modify the harness and always replace it if there is any abnormality.
Do not bend the harness to the extreme.
Do not bend the harness to the extreme.
